claimText: The Environment Audit (EA) Rules, 2025 were notified by the MoEFCC under the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
evidenceExcerpt: MoEFCC notified the Environment Audit (EA) Rules, 2025 under the Environment Protection Act (1986).
examUse: Statement-based question on the parent legislation of the EA Rules.
distractorHooks: Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974

claimText: Environmental Audit (EA) differs from Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) as EA is conducted post-establishment to check conformity to an approved Environmental Management Plan, whereas EIA is conducted prior to project establishment.
evidenceExcerpt: EA differs from EIA as EIA is conducted prior to project establishment, while EA checks conformity to the Environmental Management Plan.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing between the scope and timing of EA and EIA.
distractorHooks: EA is conducted prior to project establishment

claimText: The Environment Audit (EA) Rules, 2025 feature a Two-Tiered System comprising a government regulator review by CPCB/SPCB (Tier-1) and an Environment auditor-based mechanism (Tier-2).
evidenceExcerpt: Features a Two-Tiered System: Tier-1 (CPCB/SPCB review) and Tier-2 (Environment auditor-based mechanism).
examUse: Statement-based question on the structural tiers of the EA Rules.
distractorHooks: Tier-1 is auditor-based and Tier-2 is regulator-based

claimText: The Environment Audit Designate Agency (EADA) is responsible for the certification, registration, and oversight of Environment Auditors.
evidenceExcerpt: Environment Audit Designate Agency (EADA) is responsible for certification, registration, and oversight of Environment Auditors.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching the agency with its function.
distractorHooks: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) directly certifies auditors
Quality Council of India (QCI) is the sole designate agency

claimText: Environmental Auditing was initially introduced in India in 1992 under the Environment (Protection) Rules, requiring the submission of annual environmental statements to State Pollution Control Boards (SPCBs).
evidenceExcerpt: Introduced initially in 1992 under the Environment (Protection) Rules, requiring annual environmental statements to SPCBs.
examUse: Statement-based question on the historical evolution of environmental auditing in India.
distractorHooks: Introduced for the first time in 2025
Requires annual statements to be submitted directly to the MoEFCC
